videos.title: Davos 2025 Sprint (free) World Cup Cross country Skiing 13/12/25
videos.description:
Witness world-class speed and precision at the Davos 2025 Sprint (Free), held on 13 December 2025 as part of the Cross-Country Skiing World Cup. Set in the stunning alpine surroundings of Davos, Switzerland, this event delivers pure sprint racing at high altitude, where every second and every move matters.

The free technique sprint is one of the most explosive disciplines in cross-country skiing. Athletes must combine maximum power, flawless balance, and tactical awareness as they progress from qualification to intense head-to-head knockout heats. Davos is famous for its thin air and fast course, making endurance management just as important as raw speed.

Fans can expect thrilling moments throughout the competition—aggressive starts, daring overtakes, tight corners, and dramatic finishes that often come down to fractions of a second. The open and flowing course encourages bold racing and rewards skiers who can maintain speed while handling the physical demands of altitude.

This race is a key highlight of the mid-December World Cup calendar and provides valuable insight into sprint form as the season gains momentum. Watch as the world’s best sprinters push their limits and fight for crucial World Cup points in one of the most iconic sprint venues in the sport.
